homepage_description: SteamHistory's primary purpose is for gathering and then storing a Steam Profile's historic and or current information in a central place.

notification_dock_signin_to_vote: Sign in with Steam to vote!
notification_dock_poll_disclaimer: Polls are used to gather community thoughts and opinions, not as final decisions.
faq_q1_p1: SteamHistory is a tool for tracking Steam profile histories. You can search for any Steam user to view their past usernames, profile pictures, friends, and comments.
faq_q1_p2: Simply use the search bar on the homepage to look up a user by their SteamID64, profile URL, or custom URL.
faq_q2_title: Is logging in with Steam safe?
faq_q2_p1: Yes, logging in with Steam is safe. We use Steam's implementation of OpenID, a standard and secure authentication system.
faq_q2_p2: When you choose to log in with Steam, you are redirected to the official Steam Community website to enter your credentials. SteamHistory.net never sees or stores your password.
faq_q2_p3: After you successfully log in on the Steam website, Steam securely sends us only your unique 64-bit SteamID, which we use to identify your account. This process ensures that your login details remain private and secure.
faq_q2_p4_1: You can read more about how Steam handles third-party authentication in the official
faq_q2_p4_link: Steamworks documentation on Web Browser based authentication
faq_q3_title: How do I become a supporter?
faq_q3_p1_1: You can become a supporter either by using Ko-Fi or trading TF2 items by visiting our
faq_q3_p1_2: . Supporters get access to exclusive features like profile customization.
faq_q3_p2: Both one-time donations and memberships grant supporter perks. For one-time donations, the duration of supporter status is calculated as:
faq_q3_p3_1: Any leftover amount is stored as credit and applied to future donations. For example, donating $13 provides
faq_q3_p3_5: carried over. If the same user donates another $2 later, the credit completes another $5, granting
faq_q4_title: How do I link my supporter status from Ko-Fi?
faq_q4_p1: After donating on Ko-fi, you will receive an email from steamhistory.net with a transaction ID.
faq_q4_p2_2: and enter the email address that received the email and the transaction ID into the provided fields to link your supporter status.
faq_q5_p1: SourceBans++ is a community-driven admin, ban, and communication management system for game servers running on the Source engine. It allows server administrators to manage bans and communications across a network of servers.
faq_q5_p2: We use this data to provide additional context on user profiles for games like Team Fortress 2, Garry's Mod, CS:GO, CS2, and Counter-Strike: Source.
faq_q5_p3_1: You can find more information on the
faq_q6_p1_1: TF2 Bot Detector is a tool that automatically detects and helps votekick cheaters and bots in Team Fortress 2 casual matches. While the original project is archived, a community-maintained fork is available at
faq_q7_title: How is third party data used?
faq_q7_p1: We use third-party data from sources like SourceBans and TF2 Bot Detector to provide a more comprehensive and informative user experience. This data is displayed on user profiles to give you a better understanding of a player's history and community standing.
faq_q7_p2: For example, you can see if a user has any active bans on SourceBans-integrated servers directly on their SteamHistory profile.
faq_q8_title: What is the 'API' tool and how do I use it?
faq_q8_p1: The SteamHistory API currently provides an endpoint to retrieve all SourceBans data we have logged for a user or a list of users. This can be useful for developers who want to integrate our data into their own applications.
faq_q8_p2_1: A community-maintained fork of TF2 Bot Detector by surepy, available at
privacy_s1_p1: Welcome to SteamHistory.net. We are committed to protecting your privacy and being transparent about how we handle your data. This Privacy Policy explains what information we collect, how we use it, and what rights you have in relation to it.
privacy_s1_p2: This service is an independent project and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or in any way officially connected with Valve Corporation or Steam.
privacy_s2_p1: Our service archives publicly available information from the Steam platform and other third-party sources to provide a comprehensive history. The data we collect and display includes:
privacy_s2_l1_2: Your public Steam profile information, including your SteamID, persona name (current and historical), real name (if public), avatar (current and historical), public comments, friends list, and official ban information (VAC, game, and community bans).
privacy_s2_l2_2: We also collect ban information from third-party services such as Sourcebans and TF2BD (Team Fortress 2 Bot Detector) to provide additional context on user behavior, particularly concerning cheating and malicious activity.
privacy_s2_l3_2: If you log in to our Service via Steam, we collect and store information necessary to provide account-specific features. This includes your SteamID to manage your session, your list of "watched users," and your supporter status.
privacy_s2_l4_2: When you are logged in, you have the option to add private notes to user profiles. These notes are stored on our servers and are only visible to you. They are not shared with other users or third parties.
privacy_s2_p2: We do not collect any non-public information from your Steam account, such as your password or email address. Financial transactions for supporter status are handled by our payment processor, Ko-Fi. When you become a supporter, we receive the following information from Ko-Fi: your name, the email address you used for the transaction, the amount donated, and a unique transaction ID. We use this information solely to verify your payment, grant supporter perks to your SteamHistory account, and for internal financial record-keeping. We do not receive or store your credit card information or other sensitive payment details.
privacy_s3_p1: The information we collect is used for the following purposes:
privacy_s3_l1: To provide a historical archive of public Steam profile data for users to view.
privacy_s3_l2: To manage your user account, including providing access to supporter-only features and managing your list of watched users.
privacy_s3_l3_2: We use publicly available friends lists to reconstruct potentially private friends lists. For example, if User A has a private friends list but is friends with User B (who has a public list), we can infer that User A is friends with User B. By aggregating this information from many public profiles, we can provide a more complete picture of a user's connections.
privacy_s3_p2: Our legal basis for processing this data is our legitimate interest in operating a comprehensive archival and research service.
privacy_s4_p1: We do not sell your personal data. However, we may share information under the following circumstances:
privacy_s4_l1_2: We share information with third-party payment processors like Ko-Fi to manage supporter payments. We only share the necessary information to complete the transaction, and your payment details are handled directly by the processor.
privacy_s4_l2_2_pre: We may use third-party advertising companies, such as NitroPay, to serve ads when you visit our website. These companies may use cookies and web beacons to collect information about your visits to this and other websites in order to provide advertisements about goods and services of interest to you. For more information on how NitroPay handles privacy, please see their
privacy_s5_p1: While SteamHistory.net is not located within the European Union and does not specifically target EU residents, we honor all users' requests for data removal, irrespective of their geographic location.
privacy_s5_p2_1: Right to Erasure (Right to be Forgotten):
privacy_s5_p2_2_pre: If you wish to request the removal of your personal data from our Service, please visit
privacy_s5_p2_2_post: (login required). To ensure we are processing a request from the legitimate owner of the account, we may require you to verify your identity. The simplest way to do this is by logging into SteamHistory.net via Steam, which confirms your ownership of the account. For residents of the EU, this verification process is in accordance with GDPR Article 12, Section 6.
privacy_s5_p3: Please note that this removal process applies only to data stored on SteamHistory.net's servers. We do not have control over data held by third-party services such as Sourcebans or TF2BD. You must contact those services directly to request the removal of your data from their platforms.
privacy_s5_p4: Additionally, as outlined in our Terms of Service, we may retain non-personal data related to malicious activities for the security and protection of the service and its community.
privacy_s6_p1: Our website uses cookies. Cookies are small text files that are placed on your machine to help the site provide a better user experience. We use cookies for:
privacy_s6_l1_2: Such as keeping you logged in.
privacy_s6_l2_2: As mentioned above, third-party advertisers like NitroPay use cookies to serve personalized ads. For information about opting out of personalized advertising, please refer to the advertiser's privacy policy.
privacy_s7_p1: We retain the public data we collect for as long as it remains publicly available on the source platforms (e.g., Steam). If data is removed from the source, it may still be retained in our archives for historical purposes. If you request the removal of your data, we will delete it from our active databases.
privacy_s7_p2: For financial and legal reasons, we are required to retain transactional data from our payment processor (Ko-Fi) for a specific period. This data is kept securely and is used only for accounting and compliance purposes.
privacy_s8_p1: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Any changes will be posted on this page with an updated "Last Updated" date. You are advised to review this Privacy Policy periodically for any changes. Continued use of the service after such changes constitutes your acceptance of the new policy.
tos_s1_p1: By accessing or using SteamHistory.net (the "Service"), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service ("Terms"). If you do not agree to these Terms, you may not use the Service.
tos_s1_p2: This Service is an independent project and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or in any way officially connected with Valve Corporation or Steam.
tos_s2_p1: SteamHistory.net is a third-party service that archives and displays publicly available data from the Steam platform and other public third-party sources (such as Sourcebans and TF2BD). The purpose of the Service is to provide a historical record of public user information, including data related to cheating and malicious activity.
tos_s3_p1: You agree not to use the Service for any purpose that is illegal or prohibited by these Terms. You agree not to:
tos_s3_l1: Use the Service in any way that could damage, disable, overburden, or impair the Service.
tos_s3_l2: Attempt to gain unauthorized access to any part of the Service.
tos_s3_l3: Use any automated system, including "bots," "spiders," or "offline readers," to access the Service in a manner that sends more request messages to the servers than a human can reasonably produce in the same period by using a conventional on-line web browser.
tos_s3_l4: If you are provided with access to our API, you agree not to misuse it, including but not limited to, exceeding rate limits, attempting to reverse-engineer its functionality, or using it for any purpose that violates these Terms or is not expressly permitted.
tos_s4_p1: All data displayed on the Service, including but not limited to usernames, avatars, and comments, is the property of Valve Corporation and/or its respective users. The Steam name and logo are trademarks of Valve Corporation.
tos_s4_p2: Our Service is provided for personal, non-commercial use only. You agree not to exploit the Service or any of its parts for any commercial purpose, in accordance with the Steam Subscriber Agreement.
tos_s5_p1: We offer optional supporter tiers that provide access to enhanced features. You can become a supporter through a one-time donation or a recurring monthly subscription, managed through our third-party payment processor (e.g., Ko-Fi). By becoming a supporter, you agree to the following terms:
tos_s5_l1_2: Supporters gain access to features including, but not limited to: Multi-Source Search (searching by persona name, real name, URL, and AvatarHash), Comment Search, viewing of deleted comments, Friend Swapping (reconstructing a private friends list from public data), and cosmetic profile customizations.
tos_s5_l2_3: Donations are cumulative. For every $5 donated, you will receive 30 days of supporter access. For example, a single $15 donation grants 90 days of access.
tos_s5_l2_5: If you choose a monthly subscription, your payment will be processed automatically each month. You can cancel your subscription at any time through the payment processor's interface.
tos_s5_l3_2: All payments for supporter access, whether through one-time donations or subscriptions, are final and non-refundable.
tos_s5_l4_2: Initiating a chargeback without first contacting us to resolve a payment issue will result in a permanent ban from all SteamHistory services.
tos_s6_p1: The Service is provided on an "AS IS" and "AS AVAILABLE" basis. We make no warranties, express or implied, regarding the accuracy, reliability, or completeness of the data provided. The data is sourced from the Steam Web API and other third-party services, and we are not responsible for any errors or omissions from these sources.
tos_s6_p2: In no event shall SteamHistory.net or its operators be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages arising out of or in connection with your use of the Service.
tos_s7_p1_1: We are committed to complying with data protection laws like GDPR and honoring our users' "right to be forgotten." If you wish to request the removal of your personal data from our Service, please visit
tos_s7_p1_2: (login required), and we will process your request.
tos_s7_p2: Please note that our Service displays data from third-party sources such as Sourcebans and TF2BD. We do not control this data and cannot modify or remove it. To have such data removed, you must contact the respective third-party service directly.
tos_s7_p3: Furthermore, as a service dedicated to maintaining a historical archive for the benefit of the gaming community, particularly concerning malicious activities, we reserve the right to retain anonymized, non-personal data related to such activities for security and archival purposes. This is to protect the integrity of the Service and its users. Any such retention will be done in accordance with applicable laws.
tos_s8_p1: The Service is intended for users who are at least 13 years old. By using the Service, you represent and warrant that you are at least 13 years of age. If we become aware that a user is under 13, we will take steps to remove their information and terminate their account.
tos_s9_p1: Any disputes arising out of or in connection with these Terms or your use of the Service shall be governed by the laws of the jurisdiction in which the Service's operators are based, without regard to its conflict of law provisions. You agree to submit to the personal and exclusive jurisdiction of the courts located within that jurisdiction.
tos_s10_p1_1: Your privacy is important to us. Please review our
tos_s10_p1_2: to understand how we collect, use, and protect your information.
tos_s11_p1: We reserve the right to modify these Terms at any time. Any changes will be posted on this page with an updated "Last Updated" date. Your continued use of the Service after any such changes constitutes your acceptance of the new Terms. You are advised to review these Terms periodically for any changes.
tos_s12_p1_1: If you have any questions about these Terms, please contact us at
tos_s12_p1_2: . For requests related to data privacy and removal, please visit
supporter_subtitle: Support SteamHistory.net and unlock exclusive features to enhance your experience
supporter_f1_desc: Find comments made by a specific SteamID or containing specific words
supporter_f2_desc: Ability to view all deleted comments on a viewed profile
supporter_f3_desc: View a user's friends even if their friends list is private
supporter_f4_desc: Allows the ability to customize your SH profile in a multitude of ways, purely cosmetic
supporter_f5_desc: No on-site ads while logged in
supporter_f6_desc: Access to the tester and supporter channel for early use/discussion of WIP features
supporter_kofi_desc: Support us directly through Ko-Fi with one-time donations or monthly memberships.
supporter_duration_calc: Duration Calculation: Each $5 donated = 30 days of supporter status
supporter_bucket_system: Bucket System: Donations accumulate until they reach $5 increments
supporter_bucket_ex1: Example 1: Donate $15 = 90 days (3 months) of supporter status
supporter_bucket_ex2: Example 2: Donate $3, then later $2 = 30 days once total reaches $5
supporter_linking_p1: After donating through Ko-Fi, you'll receive an email with a transaction ID.
supporter_linking_li2: Entering your Ko-Fi email and transaction ID
supporter_linking_li3: Your supporter features will be activated immediately
